title: Kit-Free bacterial gDNA extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4aeq","text":"This is an easy and cheap protocol to extract genomic DNA from bacteria based on salt-out to remove proteins and DNA precipitation to isolate DNA from salts. Using this protocol, you should be able to extract high-molecular weight genomic DNA, free of impurities and at a high concentration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"d5ej7","text":"Preheat the heat block to 37 °C. Get some ice, and thaw reagents and enzymes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":28,"length":1}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tex_formula","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"formula":"","guid":"","id":0}}}}
steps: Pellet a 10 mL overnight bacterial culture by centrifugation with 6 000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C and decant supernatant as waste
Resuspend pellet in 1 000 µL ice-cold PBS (w/o Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ ) and transfer suspension to a 1.5 mL reaction tube
Pellet the suspension by centrifugation with 10 000 rcf for 5 minutes at room temperature and decant supernatant as waste
Resuspend pellet in 346 µL PBS (w/o Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ ), 20 µL 20 mg/mL lysozyme, 10 µL 0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.0) and 4 µL 10 mg/mL RNase A
Incubate the suspension for 30 minutes at 37°C with 250 rpm shaking
Add 50 µL 10% SDS, 10 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K and 40 µL 5 M NaCl. Mix solution by tube inversion

Incubate suspension for 30 minutes at 56°C with 2 000 rpm shaking Incubation for 30 minutes is usually enough, but if lysate is not clear (or near clear) after 30 minutes incubation, extend the incubation for several hours.
Add 360 µL 5 M NaCl and 40 µL 4 M KCl. Mix solution by tube inversion
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 5 minutes at room temperature. Decant supernatant into a 5 mL reaction tube
Incubate solution on ice for 5 minutes
Add 100 µL 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 2 500 µL ice-cold absolute ethanol
Incubate solution at -20°C for minimum 2 hours Incubate overnight to increase yield, especially important if the DNA concentration is low.
Resuspend pellet in 1 000 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol. Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 30 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Air-dry pellet until all ethanol has evaporated
Resuspend pellet in 200 µL T 10 E 0.1 buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)
Centrifuge again at 14 000 rcf for 2 minutes at 4°C. Remove residual ethanol by pipette

Resuspend pellet in 200 µL 20 mg/mL lysozyme, 196 µL lysis buffer and 4 µL 10 mg/mL RNase A 40 mL lysis buffer: - 20.0 mL 10x PBS (w/o Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ ) - 1.6 mL 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) - 0.8 mL 0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.0) - 0.8 mL 100% Triton-X - 16.8 mL distilled water
Incubate the suspension for 60 minutes at 37°C with 250 rpm shaking
Add 50 µL 10% SDS, 10 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K and 40 µL 5 M NaCl. Mix solution by tube inversion

Incubate suspension for 30 minutes at 56°C with 2 000 rpm shaking Incubation for 30 minutes is usually enough, but if lysate is not clear (or near clear) after 30 minutes incubation, extend the incubation for several hours.
Add 360 µL 5 M NaCl and 40 µL 4 M KCl. Mix solution by tube inversion
Incubate solution on ice for 5 minutes
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 5 minutes at room temperature. Decant supernatant into a 5 mL reaction tube
Add 100 µL 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 2 500 µL ice-cold 96% ethanol
Incubate solution at -20°C for minimum 2 hours Incubate overnight to increase yield, especially important if the DNA concentration is low.
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 30 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Resuspend pellet in 1 000 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol. Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Centrifuge again at 14 000 rcf for 2 minutes at 4°C. Remove residual ethanol by pipette
Air-dry pellet until all ethanol has evaporated
Resuspend pellet in 200 µL T 10 E 0.1 buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)
Resuspend pellet in 346 µL PBS (w/o Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ ), 20 µL 20 mg/mL lysozyme, 10 µL 0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.0) and 4 µL 10 mg/mL RNase A
Incubate the suspension for 60 minutes at 37°C without shaking
Add 50 µL 10% SDS, 10 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K and 40 µL 5 M NaCl. Mix solution by inverting tube 5-10 times

Incubate suspension for 30 minutes at 56°C without agitation Incubation for 30 minutes is usually enough, but if lysate is not clear (or near clear) after 30 minutes incubation, extend the incubation for several hours. Reduce agitation when incubating for a longer period.
Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 minutes at 14 000 rcf
Add 475 µL chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1). Mix by inverting tube 5-10 times
Using a 1 000 µL wide-bore pipette tip, transfer 450 µL of the upper (aqueous) phase to a new microcentrifugation tube
Add 50 µL 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 1 000 µL ice-cold absolute ethanol
Incubate solution at -20°C for minimum 2 hours Incubate overnight to increase yield, especially important if the DNA concentration is low.
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 30 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Resuspend pellet in 1 000 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol. Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Centrifuge again at 14 000 rcf for 2 minutes at 4°C. Remove residual ethanol by pipette
Air-dry pellet until all ethanol has evaporated
Resuspend pellet in 100 µL T 10 E 0.1 buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)
Using a 1 000 µL wide-bore pipette tip, transfer 475 µL of the upper (aqueous) phase to a new microcentrifugation tube
Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 minutes at 14 000 rcf
Add 500 µL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) to the sample. Mix by inverting tube 5-10 times

Resuspend pellet in 200 µL 20 mg/mL lysozyme, 196 µL lysis buffer and 4 µL 10 mg/mL RNase A 40 mL lysis buffer: - 20.0 mL 10x PBS (w/o Ca 2+ , Mg 2+ ) - 1.6 mL 1 M Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) - 0.8 mL 0.5 M EDTA (pH 8.0) - 0.8 mL 100% Triton-X - 16.8 mL distilled water
Incubate the suspension for 60 minutes at 37°C without shaking
Add 50 µL 10% SDS, 10 µL 20 mg/mL proteinase K and 40 µL 5 M NaCl. Mix solution by inverting tube 5-10 times

Incubate suspension for 30 minutes at 56°C without shaking Incubation for 30 minutes is usually enough, but if lysate is not clear (or near clear) after 30 minutes incubation, extend the incubation for several hours. Reduce agitation when incubating for a longer period.
Add 500 µL phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1) to the sample. Mix by inverting tube 5-10 times
Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 minutes at 14 000 rcf
Using a 1 000 µL wide-bore pipette tip, transfer 475 µL of the upper (aqueous) phase to a new microcentrifugation tube
Add 475 µL chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (24:1). Mix by inverting tube 5-10 times
Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 minutes at 14 000 rcf
Using a 1 000 µL wide-bore pipette tip, transfer 450 µL of the upper (aqueous) phase to a new microcentrifugation tube
Add 50 µL 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 1 000 µL ice-cold absolute ethanol
Incubate solution at -20°C for minimum 2 hours Incubate overnight to increase yield, especially important if the DNA concentration is low.
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 30 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Resuspend pellet in 1 000 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol. Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature
Centrifuge solution at 14 000 rcf for 10 minutes at 4°C. Decant supernatant as waste
Centrifuge again at 14 000 rcf for 2 minutes at 4°C. Remove residual ethanol by pipette
Air-dry pellet until all ethanol has evaporated
Resuspend pellet in 100 µL T 10 E 0.1 buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, pH 8.0)
